original_reasoning: al-Andalus was named after Vandals who conquered Iberia; clear historical connection
خشوع centers on lowering, sinking, or bodily submissive settling; lying down in a bed shares that lowered-rest posture.
Arabic refers to roots from which branches spread; Greek ethnonym can align through stock, descent, and peoplehood.
Greek means had or possessed; Arabic points to firmness, embeddedness, and settled structure, giving a plausible deep chain of holding and being fixed.
Persistent inquiry into a thing can plausibly lead to making it clear or showing it.

الصميان: انصبّ عليه / الأمر حلّ به (poured down on / the matter befell him). λαμβάνω future=about to take/receive/seize. Something befalling someone (حلّ به) parallels being seized/taken by something. The passive-reception concept connects them.
Arabic 'take and swallow' can align with the result side 'be deprived/robbed of' in Greek.

الشناط=اللحمان المنضجة / الشواء (well-cooked/roasted meats). ἀλείφω=to anoint, rub with oil/fat. Roasting meat involves fat/grease; anointing involves oil/fat. The shared medium (fat/oil on surface) makes this a plausible material-domain bridge.
